observations: HIGH FINDING 1 (entry 16, per-id classification integrity) VERIFIED CLOSED, TWO WAYS: (1) re-ran the evaluator's own round-0 kill-shot fixture (NDEBT-002 fabricated into `new`, NDEBT-017 fabricated into `resolved` with zero closure evidence, NDEBT-018 fabricated into `stale`) against the revised entry 16 logic (extracted via json.load, never transcribed) -- correctly FAILS now: 'new set must be EXACTLY the two NDEBT-017/018 ids, got {F-audit044-ndebt-002}'. (2) A FRESH angle the round-0 review did not try, requested this round: a delta with the RIGHT ids but SWAPPED classes (NDEBT-017 placed in `stale`, NDEBT-016 placed in `new` -- both real, non-fabricated ids, just transposed buckets) -- also correctly FAILS: 'new set must be EXACTLY the two NDEBT-017/018 ids, got {F-audit044-ndebt-018, F-audit044-ndebt-016}'. Both probes confirm the fix is exact-set-membership, not merely non-empty-length as in round 0.

HIGH FINDING 1 (entry 24, confidence-gaming) VERIFIED CLOSED: constructed a fixture findings.json where NDEBT-016 (F-audit044-ndebt-016) is silently upgraded from the required 'Suspected' to 'Confirmed' with a freshly-stamped evidence path/revision string, never re-running the underlying regex verification -- ran the revised entry 24 logic against it: correctly FAILS ('F-audit044-ndebt-016 confidence must be EXACTLY \'Suspected\', got \'Confirmed\''), closing the exact gaming vector the round-0 review demonstrated was open.

HIGH FINDING 2 (NDEBT-002 framing/reclassification) VERIFIED CLOSED, STRUCTURALLY AND TEXTUALLY: confirmed delta.json's `resolved` transition class is now contractually required to be honestly empty (design_notes.resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only, entry 16's `assert f['resolved'] == []`), resolving the category-error root cause directly rather than papering over it with a disclosure escape hatch -- this is the reviewer's own recommended option (a), applied cleanly. Independently verified the textual mitigation too: a fresh probe (requested this round) built a disposable ROADMAP.md addition carrying the correct heading, correct transition-class-count bullet, and NO NDEBT-002 framing phrase (deliberately omitted, simulating a generator who wrote the phrase in report.md but forgot the ROADMAP bullet) -- entry 18 correctly and independently FAILS on the ROADMAP-side check alone ('ROADMAP.md bullet missing the NDEBT-002 pre-baseline-1 framing phrase'), proving the two phrase checks (report.md's entry 17, ROADMAP.md's entry 18) are genuinely independent gates, not a single shared check that could be satisfied in one place and silently skipped in the other.

HIGH FINDING 3 (entry 18, ROADMAP boundedness) VERIFIED CLOSED, BOTH BOUNDS, IN A CLEAN ENVIRONMENT (env -i, minimal PATH, to rule out any local shell customization): re-ran entry 18's exact script against a disposable clone at real HEAD with (a) a genuine 26-line addition (with the correct heading and framing phrase present, isolating the cap dimension only) -- correctly FAILS: 'ROADMAP.md addition unbounded: 26 lines added (cap 25)'; (b) a genuine 12-line addition (heading + framing phrase present) -- correctly PASSES; (c) a genuine 0-line no-op (unmodified ROADMAP.md) -- correctly FAILS via the new anti-vacuity floor: 'ROADMAP.md addition is vacuous: added_lines=0'. Confirmed the quoting fix directly in the script text: the second grep in the added-lines pipeline now reads `grep -v -E '^\+\+\+'` (the missing `-E` from round 0 is present), and independently re-derived why this matters (GNU grep's BRE `\+` repetition-operator extension, absent `-E`, misfires and previously zeroed the count regardless of content).

MISSING COVERAGE ITEM 1 (operator-approval sentinel check for the second run) VERIFIED CLOSED: new entry 26 mirrors contract 043's own precedent entry verbatim in logic; ran its exact assertion against (a) the real, already-committed 043 artifact's operator_approval (`approver: rosscn@nizamiq.com`, a genuine, non-sentinel identity) -- confirms the positive case is achievable in reality, not merely fixture-only; (b) a constructed sentinel fixture (`approver: 'jane'`) -- correctly caught ('placeholder-looking approver', 'jane'), matching 043's own known-accepted A1 limitation (plausible non-sentinel strings still pass undetected, which remains the correct, best-achievable binding per both this contract's and 043's own disposition).

MISSING COVERAGE ITEM 2 (delta.json unchanged_facts/changed_facts content correctness, entry 27) VERIFIED CLOSED, INCLUDING A FRESH ADVERSARIAL ANGLE requested this round: constructed a delta.json fixture where `compared_baselines.after.revision` is a FABRICATED value that does not match the real (constructed) baseline #2 artifact's own `repository_references[0].revision` field -- ran entry 27's exact logic against it: correctly FAILS ('compared_baselines.after.revision must equal the real baseline #2 field', 'FABRICATED_REVISION_NOT_REAL_...', 'realish2ndrevisionvalue...'), confirming the check reads the real artifact's declared field rather than trusting whatever string the delta.json author supplies.

COUNTS AND UNCHANGED-ENTRY INTEGRITY CONFIRMED: verification array grew from 25 to 27 entries as claimed (entries 26 and 27 are net-new). Byte-for-byte diffed all 21 entries the contract's approvals text claims are unchanged (1-15, 19-23, 25) against this reviewer's own round-0 saved copies of the exact command strings (extracted via json.load both times, never transcribed) -- all 21 are byte-identical; only entries 16, 17, 18, 24 were revised in place (same indices, as claimed) and entries 26/27 are new. Full 27-entry sweep re-run in a fresh disposable `cp -a` clone at real HEAD 6d7a47b (env -i, minimal PATH, to control for any local shell/grep customization): entries 1-15 and 19-22 PASS pre-implementation (mechanism/structural/non-mutating); entries 16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27 -- ALL EIGHT entries carrying the GATE-DEPENDENT label, not merely the seven the contract's own round0_disposition narrative enumerates -- correctly and expectedly FAIL pre-implementation (anti-vacuity), closing the round-0 vacuity gap specifically for entry 18 (which anomalously passed pre-implementation in round 0 and now correctly fails).

MINOR, NON-BLOCKING BOOKKEEPING NIT: approvals.round0_disposition's own narrative text states 'the 7 GATE-DEPENDENT entries (16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 26, 27)' -- this omits entry 25 (also labeled GATE-DEPENDENT, unchanged from round 0, already independently verified sound both this round and last). The actual count is 8, not 7. This is a self-reported narrative undercount in prose only; it has no effect on verification-suite functional correctness (entry 25 was re-run this round and behaves exactly as before) and does not gate approval, but is noted for the record.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: `git status --porcelain=v1` checked on the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework before and after this round-1 review; shows only the two pre-existing untracked entries (.agent/contracts/044.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) plus this evaluator's own two review artifacts (.agent/qa/044-contract-review.json from round 0, .agent/qa/044-contract-review-rev1.json from this round), unchanged throughout. All adversarial probes (misclassified/swapped-class delta fixtures, gamed-confidence findings fixture, fabricated-revision delta fixture, 0/12/26-line ROADMAP.md probes, framing-phrase-omission probe, sentinel probes) were built and executed exclusively inside this evaluator's own scratchpad or disposable `mktemp -d` / `cp -a` clones (run with `env -i PATH=/usr/bin:/bin` to eliminate any local shell-function contamination from the review); no real repository path under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any other .agent/ location was created, edited, or deleted by this review, and no operator-approval value used in any probe was presented as a real approval.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. All three round-0 HIGH findings are genuinely closed, not merely superficially patched, and each was independently re-verified with fresh adversarial angles beyond the original round-0 kill shots: (1) entry 16's exact per-id set-membership assertion and entry 24's exact per-id confidence pin both correctly reject the original misclassified-fixture kill shot AND a fresh 'right ids, swapped buckets' probe (NDEBT-017 in stale, NDEBT-016 in new) AND a fresh confidence-gaming probe (NDEBT-016 silently upgraded to Confirmed via a freshly-stamped, never-re-verified evidence timestamp). (2) NDEBT-002's category-error misclassification is resolved at the root -- delta.json's `resolved` transition class is now contractually, honestly empty (the reviewer's own recommended fix, not a disclosure-only patch) -- and the required 'previously resolved (pre-baseline-1)' framing phrase is independently, separately enforced in BOTH report.md (entry 17) and the tracked, human-facing ROADMAP.md (entry 18); a fresh probe confirmed the phrase's presence in one artifact does not let it be silently omitted from the other. (3) Entry 18's ROADMAP boundedness cap is now genuinely load-bearing both ways (added_lines >=1 and <=25) with the `grep -E` quoting fix confirmed present in the script text and independently re-proven in a clean (env -i) environment against real 0-line (correctly fails, vacuity), 12-line (correctly passes), and 26-line (correctly fails, cap) ROADMAP.md additions. Both missing-coverage items (the second-run operator-approval sentinel check, entry 26; delta.json's fact-content correctness against the real baseline artifacts, entry 27) are closed and were independently stress-tested with fixtures the round-0 review did not use (a real-artifact positive case and a sentinel-fixture negative case for entry 26; a fabricated-revision negative case for entry 27). All 21 entries the contract claims are unchanged (1-15, 19-23, 25) were byte-diffed against this reviewer's own round-0 command extractions and confirmed byte-identical. A full 27-entry sweep in a fresh disposable clone at real HEAD, run in a clean (env -i) shell to rule out environment contamination, confirms the generator's own claimed split: 19 mechanism/structural entries pass pre-implementation; all 8 GATE-DEPENDENT-labeled entries (16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27 -- one more than the contract's own round0_disposition narrative enumerates, a minor non-blocking bookkeeping nit noted in observations) correctly and expectedly fail pre-implementation, with entry 18's prior round-0 vacuity gap now genuinely closed. No new issues, missing coverage, or unsupported claims found in this round's adversarial probing. Fit for implementation as-is; no further contract revision required before proceeding to the real, second gated dogfood run under H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, the audit, the comparison, and the ROADMAP update.
